title: Charred Molten Chinese Pork Braised Jjigae
description: A rich and savory monsoon-inspired Chinese-Xinjiang dish featuring ground pork, a sticky glaze, and a medley of spices, all presented in the form of Buddha jumping over the wall. This vegetarian-friendly jjigae is designed to warm the senses and nourish the soul.
instructions: Heat sesame oil in a large wok or Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Add the ground pork and cook, breaking it up with a spoon, until it is no longer pink, about 5 minutes.
Add the ginger and garlic and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the soy sauce, hoisin sauce, brown sugar, Korean chili flakes, and five-spice powder, and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to medium-low and simmer, stirring occasionally, for 10 minutes.
Mix the cornstarch with a little water until smooth, then stir it into the pork mixture and cook for 2 minutes, or until the sauce has thickened.
Transfer the pork mixture to a large serving dish and top with sliced green onions and toasted sesame seeds.
Caramelize the glaze by heating the remaining 2 tablespoons of sesame oil in a small saucepan over medium heat.
Add the cornstarch mixture to the saucepan and cook, stirring constantly, for 2-3 minutes, or until the glaze is thick and bubbly.
Pour the glaze over the pork mixture and serve immediately.
Serve hot, garnished with additional green onions and sesame seeds if desired.
